name: Fire Deities
shortDescription: Fire deities in Celtic mythology represent the power, warmth, and transformative aspects of fire, often associated with craftsmanship, healing, and inspiration.
description: Fire held a significant place in Celtic culture, not only for practical purposes like warmth and cooking but also as a powerful symbol of purification, transformation, and inspiration. Consequently, various deities were associated with fire, embodying different aspects of its nature. Brigid is perhaps the most prominent fire deity in Celtic mythology, often linked to the hearth, poetry, healing, and smithcraft. While not exclusively a fire deity, her association with these domains strongly connects her to the element. Other figures, such as smith gods like Goibhniu, are also implicitly linked to fire due to their craft requiring intense heat and skill in manipulating metal. The importance of fire festivals, like Beltane, further underscores the reverence for fire in Celtic traditions. These festivals involved ritual bonfires, symbolizing purification and renewal, and connecting the community to the power of the sun and its life-giving energy. Fire deities were not merely representations of destructive force; they were also guardians of knowledge, creativity, and the domestic sphere, embodying the vital flame that sustained life and culture.

sources.title: Myths and Symbols in Pagan Europe
sources.author: H.R. Ellis Davidson
